stories.title: Two blazing quasars caught waltzing into a merger
bullets: Astronomers using ALMA confirmed a quasar pair, J2037–4537, in merging galaxies at redshift 5.7, dating back to the early universe.

stories.title: 60 Bougie Things For Men That Are Actually So Useful
bullets: A family reflects on their recovery one year after their toddler was nearly killed in the Lapu-Lapu Day tragedy.
The incident involved an SUV crashing into festival-goers, resulting in multiple injuries and one fatality.
The family's ongoing struggles highlight the long-term impact of such traumatic events on victims and their loved ones.

stories.title: Hormuz crisis throws spotlight on world's largest 'chokepoint' — the Malacca Strait
bullets: The Malacca Strait, crucial for global trade, carries nearly 22% of the world's maritime traffic, connecting East Asia to Europe and the Middle East.
Recent tensions in the Strait have raised concerns over shipping security, impacting trade routes and potentially increasing shipping costs.
As the world's largest chokepoint, disruptions here could lead to significant economic consequences, affecting global supply chains and energy prices.

stories.title: Alleged White House Correspondents’ Dinner Shooter Was An Indie Games Developer
bullets: Cole Tomas Allen, an indie game developer, has been identified as the alleged shooter at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 26, 2026.
Allen previously released one game on Steam and was working on another; he faces serious charges following the incident.
This revelation raises questions about mental health and the influence of gaming culture on violent behavior in society.

stories.title: ‘He refused to say that there was no radiation and no danger to the population'
bullets: April 26 marks the anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster, which occurred in 1986, affecting millions in Ukraine and beyond.
Dr. Andrei Kindzelski, son of a key radiologist, reflects on the lasting impact of the disaster and the ongoing health concerns.
The anniversary serves as a reminder of nuclear safety and the importance of transparency in public health communications.

stories.title: Lego replaced with pasta in $34k return scam
bullets: Jerrelle Augustine, 34, scammed $34,000 by returning boxes of Lego bricks swapped for pasta at stores across California.
He was caught after a police investigation revealed his fraudulent return scheme, which involved over 100 transactions across multiple stores.
Augustine now faces felony charges, and this case highlights vulnerabilities in retail return policies that could prompt stricter regulations.

stories.title: ARC Raiders Riven Tides countdown: Exact date and time
bullets: The Riven Tides update for ARC Raiders is set to launch on April 28, featuring a new map and gameplay enhancements.
Embark Studios confirmed the update will include new missions and features, aiming to boost player engagement and retention.
This update is part of a series of planned enhancements, indicating the developers' commitment to evolving the game based on player feedback.

stories.title: Wellinton Water reports 'Minor discharge' of wastewater into Lyall Bay
bullets: Wellinton Water reported a minor discharge of wastewater into Lyall Bay due to a blockage in the 150mm CIRC inlet main.
The incident raised environmental concerns, prompting immediate investigations and potential fines for the water services provider.
This discharge could affect local marine life and water quality, emphasizing the need for improved infrastructure and maintenance.

stories.title: Ōhura's state of emergency lifts, but locals face long road to recovery
bullets: Ōhura's state of emergency was lifted after severe flooding last week, but many residents are left with significant property damage.
Local officials reported that numerous families have lost everything, with recovery efforts expected to take months or longer.
The community is now focusing on rebuilding, which will require substantial financial aid and support from government agencies.

stories.title: curved shell-like facades enfold montpellier residential units by vincent callebaut
bullets: Vincent Callebaut's new residential units in Montpellier feature innovative curved shell-like facades designed to enhance communal living.
The project emphasizes sustainability, incorporating shared terraces and loggias to foster community interaction among residents.
This design approach reflects a growing trend in urban architecture, prioritizing both aesthetics and environmental considerations.

stories.title: 1 reason why healthy people have heart attacks, and what experts say could help
bullets: Research shows that about 20% of Canadians have elevated Lipoprotein(a) levels, increasing their heart attack risk without symptoms.
Currently, there is no routine screening for Lp(a), leaving many unaware of their risk factors for cardiovascular diseases.
Advocates are pushing for awareness and testing to prevent heart attacks, highlighting the need for better public health initiatives.

stories.title: All songs in Yunyun Syndrome Rhythm Psychosis
bullets: Yunyun Syndrome Rhythm Psychosis features over 10 tracks in its full version, expanding from the demo's offerings.
The game includes music from various artists, enhancing gameplay with diverse auditory experiences for players.
Future downloadable content (DLC) will allow players to further expand their track lists, keeping the game fresh and engaging.

stories.title: King’s Day 2026: tighter rules, fines and a Frisian visit
bullets: The Netherlands prepares for King's Day on April 27, celebrating King Willem-Alexander's 59th birthday with nationwide festivities.
This year, tighter rules and fines will be enforced to ensure safety and order during the celebrations, impacting public gatherings.
The event is a significant cultural celebration, showcasing Dutch heritage and community spirit, drawing large crowds across the country.

stories.title: Auckland boy racer manslaughter convict Micheal Ngamo named, prison sentence lengthened
bullets: Micheal Ngamo, convicted of manslaughter in a boy racer incident, had his prison sentence lengthened following a recent court ruling.
The victim's family expressed devastation, stating, 'This offence uprooted my entire life,' highlighting the tragedy's impact.
This case underscores ongoing concerns about street racing and public safety, prompting calls for stricter enforcement against reckless driving.

stories.title: Aussie stuns Brazil's fab four for first World Surf League title
bullets: Australian surfer George Pittar won his first World Surf League title at the Margaret River Pro on April 26, 2026.
Pittar defeated four Brazilian world champions, showcasing exceptional skill and determination throughout the competition.
This victory marks a significant milestone in Pittar's career, elevating his status in the global surfing community.

stories.title: Fish oil may be hurting your brain, new study finds
bullets: A new study reveals that fish oil may harm the brains of individuals with repeated mild head injuries, published on April 26, 2026.
Researchers found that EPA, a key omega-3 fatty acid in fish oil, disrupts healing and contributes to cognitive decline.
These findings could reshape dietary recommendations for concussion recovery and brain health management.

stories.title: Trump security threat live updates: President evacuated from White House dinner after shots fired
bullets: President Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 26, 2026, after shots were fired nearby.
Security footage showed a suspect rushing a checkpoint; no injuries were reported, and the suspect was apprehended.
This incident raises concerns about security at high-profile events and the safety of public figures.

stories.title: The real skills crisis is executive imagination
bullets: A recent article argues that the real skills crisis lies in executive imagination, published on April 26, 2026.
Leaders are urged to reinvent and adapt to AI advancements rather than solely focus on retraining workers.
This perspective emphasizes the need for innovative leadership to navigate evolving market demands.

stories.title: LIVE:  ABC News Live
bullets: ABC News is providing 24/7 coverage of breaking news and live events as of April 25, 2026.
The platform aims to keep viewers informed with real-time updates and comprehensive reporting.
This initiative reflects the growing demand for immediate news access in today's fast-paced environment.

stories.title: Trump holds press briefing after shooting outside White House Correspondents' Dinner
bullets: President Trump held a press briefing on April 26, 2026, following a shooting incident outside the Correspondents' Dinner.
FBI Director Kash Patel and Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche joined Trump to address the situation and security measures.
The briefing highlighted the administration's commitment to ensuring safety at public events amid rising security threats.

stories.title: As Walk for Peace begins in Sri Lanka, activists call for animal rights
bullets: A Walk for Peace began in Sri Lanka on April 26, 2026, led by 24 Buddhist monks promoting compassion and nonviolence.
The monks are accompanied by Aloka, a stray dog, symbolizing the movement's message of peace and animal rights.
This initiative aims to raise awareness about social issues and foster a culture of kindness in Sri Lanka.

stories.title: BYD's next all-electric hypercar is a convertible that's coming to Europe first
bullets: BYD unveiled the Denza Z, an all-electric hypercar, at the Beijing Auto Show on April 25, 2026.
The hypercar boasts over 1,000 horsepower and can accelerate from 0 to 60 mph in under two seconds.
This launch signifies BYD's expansion into the luxury electric vehicle market, competing with established brands.

stories.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman apologizes for not reporting ChatGPT account of Tumbler Ridge suspect to police
bullets: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, apologized on April 25, 2026, for not reporting alarming ChatGPT conversations linked to Tumbler Ridge shooter Jesse Van Rootselaar.
The account was banned in June for policy violations, but Altman acknowledged the failure to alert law enforcement about potential violence.
This incident raises questions about AI accountability and the responsibilities of tech companies in preventing real-world harm.

stories.title: This Buzzy Pocket-Sized E-Reader Is Under $60 on Amazon for the Next Few Hours
bullets: A pocket-sized e-reader is available for under $60 on Amazon for a limited time, as of April 25, 2026.
Despite its small size and some design flaws, it offers excellent value for budget-conscious readers.
This promotion highlights the growing popularity of e-readers as affordable alternatives to traditional books.

stories.title: Militants and separatists launch coordinated attacks across Mali
bullets: On April 25, 2026, militants and separatists launched coordinated attacks across Mali, including Bamako's international airport.
The al-Qaida-linked group JNIM claimed responsibility for the strikes, which targeted multiple cities simultaneously.
These attacks represent one of the largest coordinated assaults in Mali in recent years, escalating regional instability.

stories.title: Google Wallet adds support for three more digital passport IDs on Android
bullets: Google Wallet now supports digital passport IDs from Canada, Australia, and the UK as of April 25, 2026.
This update enhances user convenience, allowing seamless travel with digital IDs, which could reduce physical document reliance.
The expansion aims to increase Google Wallet's adoption in international travel, potentially impacting competitors in the digital ID space.

stories.title: Trump Cancels Iran Peace Talks at Last Minute: 'We Have All the Cards'
bullets: Donald Trump canceled Iran peace talks scheduled for April 25, 2026, stating, 'We have all the cards' in a strategic maneuver.
This marks the second cancellation of a high-level U.S. mission to Pakistan within a week, indicating shifting diplomatic priorities.
The cancellation could impact U.S.-Iran relations, complicating efforts for peace in the region amid ongoing tensions.
